title: Authors and Citation

title: Authors and Citation
text: Irvine (2026). mc: Mail Composer - Email Composition Delivery. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://github.com/NewGraphEnvironment/mc.
code: @Manual{,   title = {mc: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery},   author = {Allan Irvine},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/NewGraphEnvironment/mc}, }

what: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
title: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
text: Mail Composer. Three lines draft email. Compose, draft, send emails markdown files via Gmail API. Wraps gmailr eliminate 80 lines boilerplate every email script repeats.

title: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
text: Write email body markdown. Everything --- separator human-readable envelope (notes author) stripped mc_md_render() conversion. Recipients, subject, envelope fields set R parameters mc_send(). Send three lines: creates Gmail draft HTML formatting standard signature appended. ’re ready send real:
code: # Email to Brandon - Cottonwood  **Subject:** Cottonwood plugs - 2026 planting **To:** brandon@example.com  ---  Hi Brandon,  Quick question about the cottonwood plugs.  Thanks, Al library(mc) mc_auth() mc_send("communications/draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Cottonwood plugs - 2026 planting") mc_send("communications/draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Cottonwood plugs",         draft = FALSE)

title: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
text: Reply existing conversation: Note: gm_create_draft() support thread_id. Drafts always standalone. Use draft = FALSE send directly thread, send draft manually Gmail UI (Gmail match subject line starts “Re:”).
code: # Find the thread mc_thread_find("from:brandon subject:cottonwood")  # Send into it mc_send("draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Re: Cottonwood plugs",         thread_id = "19c05f0a98188c91",         draft = FALSE)

title: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
text: Send preview: Test mode redirects address, strips CC/BCC, ignores thread_id.
code: mc_send("draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Cottonwood plugs",         test = TRUE)

title: Mail Composer - Email Composition and Delivery
text: MIT

title: Authenticate with Gmail — mc_auth
text: Wrapper around gmailr::gm_auth() default New Graph email address. Call per session mc_send().

title: Authenticate with Gmail — mc_auth
Authenticate with Gmail — mc_auth
text: email Email address authenticate . Default "al@newgraphenvironment.com".

title: Authenticate with Gmail — mc_auth
text: Invisible NULL. Called side effect authenticating.

title: Authenticate with Gmail — mc_auth

what: Render a markdown draft to HTML email body — mc_md_render
title: Render a markdown draft to HTML email body — mc_md_render
text: Reads markdown file, strips everything first --- separator (header metadata), converts remaining markdown HTML, inlines table styles Gmail compatibility, appends New Graph signature.

title: Render a markdown draft to HTML email body — mc_md_render
Render a markdown draft to HTML email body — mc_md_render
text: path Path markdown draft file. sig Logical. Append standard signature? Default TRUE.

title: Render a markdown draft to HTML email body — mc_md_render
text: character string HTML ready mc_send().

title: Render a markdown draft to HTML email body — mc_md_render
text: Gmail strips <style> blocks HTML emails. function adds inline styles <table>, <th>, <td> elements tables render correctly. markdown file follow compost template format:   Everything including --- line stripped. Everything converted HTML.
code: # Email to Recipient - Topic  **Subject:** ...  **To:** ...  ---  Hi Name,  Body text here.

title: Render a markdown draft to HTML email body — mc_md_render

what: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
text: main function. Reads markdown draft, renders HTML, builds MIME message, either creates Gmail draft sends immediately.

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
code: mc_send(   path = NULL,   to,   subject,   cc = NULL,   from = "al@newgraphenvironment.com",   thread_id = NULL,   draft = TRUE,   test = FALSE,   sig = TRUE,   html = NULL )

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
text: path Path markdown draft file. Passed mc_md_render(). Recipient email address (character string vector). subject Email subject line. cc Optional CC recipients (character vector). Default NULL. Sender address. Default "al@newgraphenvironment.com". thread_id Gmail thread ID reply . Default NULL (new thread). Use mc_thread_find() look thread IDs. draft Logical. TRUE (default), create Gmail draft. FALSE, send immediately. test Logical. TRUE, override (send self) ignore cc thread_id. Default FALSE. sig Logical. Append signature? Passed mc_md_render(). Default TRUE. html Optional pre-rendered HTML body. provided, path ignored HTML used directly.

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
text: Invisible NULL. Prints status message.

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
text: Authenticates automatically active Gmail session detected.

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
text: Gmail's gm_create_draft() support thread_id. draft = TRUE thread_id set, mc_send() issues warning draft appear thread manually sent Gmail UI. Set subject "Re: Original Subject" Gmail's thread-matching heuristic can place correctly. draft = FALSE thread_id set, message sent directly thread via gm_send_message(thread_id = ...).

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
text: test = TRUE sends , strips CC, ignores thread_id prevent accidental sends real threads development.

title: Send or draft an email from a markdown file — mc_send
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create a draft (safe default) mc_send("communications/draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Cottonwood plugs")  # Send into an existing thread mc_send("communications/draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Re: Cottonwood plugs",         thread_id = "19c05f0a98188c91",         draft = FALSE)  # Test mode — sends to self mc_send("communications/draft.md",         to = "brandon@example.com",         subject = "Cottonwood plugs",         test = TRUE) } # }

what: Return the standard New Graph email signature as HTML — mc_sig
title: Return the standard New Graph email signature as HTML — mc_sig
text: Reads signature template bundled inst/sig/signature.html. Used mc_md_render() append signature automatically.

title: Return the standard New Graph email signature as HTML — mc_sig
Return the standard New Graph email signature as HTML — mc_sig
text: character string HTML.

title: Return the standard New Graph email signature as HTML — mc_sig

what: Search Gmail for thread IDs — mc_thread_find
title: Search Gmail for thread IDs — mc_thread_find
text: Searches Gmail messages returns matching thread IDs. Useful finding thread_id pass mc_send() replying existing conversation.

title: Search Gmail for thread IDs — mc_thread_find
Search Gmail for thread IDs — mc_thread_find
text: query Gmail search query. Supports syntax Gmail search box (e.g., ":brandon subject:cottonwood"). n Maximum number results. Default 5.

title: Search Gmail for thread IDs — mc_thread_find
text: data frame columns thread_id, , subject, date, sorted recent first.

title: Search Gmail for thread IDs — mc_thread_find
